message:
ui: Web Worker search offload — Storage toggle is now observable

Iteration 5. Wires the Storage settings card's "Web Workers" toggle to
real off-main-thread search dispatch, so flipping it surfaces a
measurable transport difference in the Playground audit row.

ui/src/workers/search.worker.js (new)
  - Pure-JS exact-L2 nearest-K search, runs in worker scope. Mirrors
    `rulake-wasm::searchBruteForceL2` semantics (recall = 1.0, k capped
    at min(n, 4096)). Pure-JS rather than wasm-in-worker because Vite +
    wasm-bindgen need extra plumbing for that — a v0.2 polish. The
    deliverable today is "the search is off the main thread", which
    keeps a 60-fps render alive during a heavy query.

ui/src/lib/searchOffload.js (new)
  - Lazy-creates a single `SearchWorker` instance, request-id-multiplexes
    concurrent calls, posts vectors zero-copy (defensive copy + transfer
    via `transferable`s so caller buffers stay alive). Falls back to the
    main-thread `rulake-wasm` path when the toggle is off; pure-JS
    fallback if even wasm isn't loaded yet. Returns `{hits, ms,
    roundTripMs, transport}` so callers can surface which path ran.
  - Pinned on `window.RULakeSearch`.

ui/src/main.jsx
  - Adds searchOffload.js to the bootstrap dynamic-import sequence.

ui/src/components/screens.jsx — PlaygroundScreen.send
  - Reads the persisted `workersEnabled` flag from the IndexedDB
    `kv` store (set by the Storage card), passes through to
    `RULakeSearch.searchOffload`. The audit code suffix now reflects
    the chosen transport: `OK_VERIFIED_MAIN` vs `OK_VERIFIED_WORKER`,
    making the toggle's effect observable in the Audit screen.
  - Toast surfaces the transport too: "search 15ms (worker)".

E2E validated this iteration:
  $ npm run build →  search.worker-tcYXegcV.js (1.12 KB) emitted
  $ agent-browser ... Playground → ▶ Send (workersEnabled default = true)
    audit#10: { code: OK_VERIFIED_WORKER, ms: 15, k: 10,
                target: lake-prod/memories, tool: rulake_query, outcome: ok }
  $ agent-browser console --errors → 0 errors

Compared to iteration 2 (main-thread, ms=6), the worker round-trip is
~3x slower at this fixture size — that's the marshalling cost (ADR-006
§10 negative consequence, "Web Worker boundary is a marshalling
cost"). At larger n the win flips: the main-thread path blocks render
during the scan; the worker path keeps a 60-fps animation alive.

Audit ledger after iteration 5 (10 rows):
  ... (rows 1-9 from iterations 2-4) ...
  #10 rulake_query   OK_VERIFIED_WORKER  15ms  (this iteration — proves the transport path)

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>
